file_name: XACCT Technologies, Inc.SUPPORT AND MAINTENANCE AGREEMENT.txt

perturbation.original_text: 1.5 "Product Maintenance" means the Product updates and revisions that are available from XACCT and selected by Licensee, as further referenced herein and the attachments hereto as may be amended from time to time.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.5 "Product Maintenance" means the Product updates and revisions that are automatically provided by XACCT, regardless of Licensee selection, and as further referenced herein and the attachments hereto as may be amended from time to time.
perturbation.explanation: This change alters the definition of 'Product Maintenance'. Originally, updates were selected by the Licensee. Now, updates are automatically provided by XACCT, regardless of the Licensee's preferences. This creates an in-text contradiction with Section 2, which states that 'Licensee may purchase the level of Product Maintenance and Support Services set forth in Attachment 1 to this Agreement,' implying a choice in what is purchased. It is also conflicting with the meaning of Incident as the Licensee doesn't have any control over the updates being given to them.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Licensee may purchase the level of Product Maintenance and Support Services set forth in Attachment 1 to this Agreement. XACCT will provide the Product Maintenance and Support Services purchased by Licensee subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement and the License Agreement. Fees shall be payable within thirty (30) days of invoice which shall be exclusive of any applicable local, state, federal, use, excise, value added, GST or other taxes imposed on the fees payable for the Product Maintenance and Support Services which shall be the responsibility of Licensee.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Product Maintenance', stating that updates are automatically provided regardless of Licensee selection. This contradicts the original meaning and directly conflicts with the 'Contradicted Text', which states that 'Licensee may purchase the level of Product Maintenance', implying a choice. The change removes the Licensee's option to choose, creating a clear inconsistency that would likely cause confusion regarding the Licensee's obligations and XACCT's responsibilities. The modification removes the licensee's agency and breaks the original contract.

perturbation.original_text: 3.1 XACCT will provide reasonable commercial efforts to provide the appropriate solutions for reported Incidents.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.1 XACCT is not obligated to provide any solutions for reported Incidents.
perturbation.explanation: This change contradicts the initial promise of XACCT providing solutions for reported incidents. This directly opposes the term 'Incident' under section 1.2, as Incidents are malfunctions that require more than one response, by removing any obligation, this contradicts the meaning of 'incident' itself.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.2 "Incident" means a single, discrete, malfunction or other problem which may require more than one (1) response before it is closed.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that XACCT is 'not obligated to provide any solutions', which directly contradicts section 1.2 defining 'Incident' as a malfunction requiring 'more than one response'. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because the definition of an 'Incident' inherently requires a response, which the modified section eliminates, rendering the term meaningless and unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: The Agreement will be automatically renewed for additional one (1) year terms (subject to applicable fee adjustments) unless thirty (30) days prior to the anniversary of the Effective Date Licensee gives written notice to XACCT of its intention not to renew.
perturbation.changed_text: The Agreement will be automatically renewed for additional one (1) year terms regardless of any notice provided by the Licensee.
perturbation.explanation: This change removes the Licensee's ability to prevent automatic renewal. It contradicts the original intent by making the renewal automatic regardless of Licensee's intention. This introduces an in-text contradiction with Section 5.2, which discusses termination initiated by the Licensee for breach of contract, as the agreement is automatically renewed. It can also create issues with Section 6 regarding reistatement of services.
perturbation.contradicted_text: XACCT may suspend or terminate Product Maintenance and Support Services if Licensee fails to timely pay Product Maintenance and Support Service fees as provided in this Agreement. XACCT may also terminate Support Services if Licensee breaches any provision of this Agreement or the License Agreement and such breach is not remedied within thirty (30) days after Licensee receives written notice of the breach. XACCT shall also have the right not to renew this Agreement with respect to any Product by providing written notice of such election at least sixty (60) days prior to the termination of Support Services for such Product, provided that XACCT no longer generally provides Support Services for such Product, or no longer provides the specific Support Services previously offered.
perturbation.justification: The modified text, by removing the Licensee's ability to prevent automatic renewal, creates a direct conflict with the contradicted text. The contradicted text explicitly states that XACCT has the right not to renew the agreement, which implies the Licensee also has a say in renewal, whereas the changed text states that the renewal is automatic 'regardless of any notice provided by the Licensee.' This creates an in-text contradiction regarding the renewal terms and potentially impacts the termination clauses, in line with the definition of 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction'.
